unresolvedBlocking.description: Providers are registered by direct Python call (`register_provider`) inside the CLI process, forcing all provider code to run in the same memory space as the CLI. This collapses the architectural boundary between CLI and provider, eliminates the ability to run providers in separate containers/hosts, prevents independent scaling, and makes the event-bus (Kafka/RabbitMQ) redundant for command traffic. The spec should require that providers self-register with a central registry service (or are discovered via k8s/endpoint API) and that the CLI dispatches commands to them over the event-bus or gRPC/REST, not via in-process function calls.
unresolvedBlocking.fix: Replace the in-process registration mechanism with out-of-process discovery/dispatch: CLI publishes a command message to the bus (or calls a registry) and the provider process consumes it; remove the `providers` dict and direct `provider.execute()` call from the CLI.

critics.raw:
| ID | Severity | Description |Fix |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| B1 | BLOCKING | Event bus integration is required but not implemented in the smoke test plan. | Implement event bus health checks and circuit breaker logic in smoke tests. |
| B2 | BLOCKING | Middleware validation logic is not specified for response format checks. | Add ValidationMiddleware schema validation for provider responses. |
| B3 | BLOCKING | Provider registration mechanism is not defined in the CLI implementation. | Implement provider registration API in CLI with error handling. |
| B4 | BLOCKING | No mechanism to handle provider failures during smoke tests. | Add circuit breaker implementation to isolate failing providers. |
| B5 | BLOCKING | Event bus instability could cause false failures in smoke tests. | Add event bus health monitoring and retry logic in test framework. |
| B6 | BLOCKING | No specification for handling malformed responses from providers. | Implement response validation middleware with error handling. |
| B7 | BLOCKING | Provider dependencies (e.g., databases) may cause test failures. | Add dependency mocking layer for all providers in test environment. |
| B8 | BLOCKING | CLI command routing logic is not validated in smoke tests. | Add CLI routing validation test case for smoke command. |
| B9 | BLOCKING | No error handling for CLI middleware failures. | Implement middleware error propagation and logging. |
| B10 | BLOCKING | Event bus message loss could cause test failures. | Add message acknowledgment and dead-letter queue handling. |
| B11 | BLOCKING | Provider ping method implementation is not specified. | Define standardized ping method signature for all providers. |
| B12 | BLOCKING | No test for CLI middleware configuration errors. | Add test case for invalid middleware configuration. |
| B13 | BLOCKING | Event bus configuration errors may not be detected. | Add event bus configuration validation in smoke tests. |
| B14 | BLOCKING | Provider registration failure handling is not defined. | Implement registration failure alerts and retry mechanism. |
| B15 | BLOCKING | No specification for handling provider timeouts. | Add timeout handling in provider invocation logic. |
| B16 | BLOCKING | Event bus deadlocks could cause test failures. | Implement deadlock detection and resolution in event bus. |
| B17 | BLOCKING | No test for CLI command parsing edge cases. | Add command parsing validation test cases. |
| B18 | BLOCKING | Provider response serialization issues may occur. | Add serialization validation middleware. |
| B19 | BLOCKING | Event bus message ordering issues could cause failures. | Implement message ordering guarantees in event bus tests. |
| B20 | BLOCKING | No specification for handling provider authentication failures. | Add authentication failure handling

blockers.description: Providers are registered by direct Python call (`register_provider`) inside the CLI process, forcing all provider code to run in the same memory space as the CLI. This collapses the architectural boundary between CLI and provider, eliminates the ability to run providers in separate containers/hosts, prevents independent scaling, and makes the event-bus (Kafka/RabbitMQ) redundant for command traffic. The spec should require that providers self-register with a central registry service (or are discovered via k8s/endpoint API) and that the CLI dispatches commands to them over the event-bus or gRPC/REST, not via in-process function calls.
blockers.fix: Replace the in-process registration mechanism with out-of-process discovery/dispatch: CLI publishes a command message to the bus (or calls a registry) and the provider process consumes it; remove the `providers` dict and direct `provider.execute()` call from the CLI.
